rationale: Trump's threat to seize Iran's Kharg Island — the world's largest oil export terminal — represents a qualitative escalation in the US-Iran conflict with direct implications for global energy prices and thus Polish fuel/energy costs.

top_events.title: Trump threatens to seize Iran's Kharg Island
top_events.summary: Trump stated the US 'will be taking' Kharg Island, Iran's main oil export hub handling ~90% of its crude exports. This is a qualitative escalation beyond the ongoing strike exchanges — if acted upon, it would cause a major oil price shock directly affecting Polish fuel and energy prices.

top_events.title: US-Iran strikes continue for second day
top_events.summary: US and Iran exchanged strikes across the Middle East for a second consecutive day. This is a continuation of the known conflict — no qualitative change beyond what was already priced in as background energy risk for Poland.

top_events.title: Pentagon hazardous materials incident
top_events.summary: Firefighters responded to a hazardous materials incident at the Pentagon. Details are sparse; if it were a serious attack it would be highly significant, but current reporting suggests a contained incident with no confirmed threat to NATO operations.
